sections.paragraph: ἀγνοέω [ἀ-priv., νοέω] ‘to be without knowledge of someth.’, be ignorant, be uninformed Ro 2:4; Hb 5:2 (of sinning without awareness that one has made a mistake). W. neg.: 2 Cor 2:11; difft. Ro 1:13, where the neg. goes with θέλω; in Mk 9:32 the negated verb produces the sense not understand. In 1 Cor 14:38 the verb in context signifies disregard, suggesting willful, and therefore culpable ignorance.
